历年位次与招生计划.title: Historical Rankings and Enrollment Plan
历年位次与招生计划.text: From 2021 to 2025, the minimum ranking for Physics Category was approximately 3922 → 2043 → 1931 → 1494 → 1269, showing a continuous upward trend (ranking numbers decreasing year by year). Over the past 4 years, the ranking has moved forward by about 2653 places, with an average annual forward movement of about 663 places. The 2025 baseline is approximately 1269, the highest historical level. The university's enrollment plan for Physics Category in Hunan Province was 109 in 2021, 110 in 2022, 103 in 2023, 103 in 2024, 98 in 2025, and 98 in 2026. The 2026 plan is the same as 2025, with no expansion or reduction.

学校层次与声誉.title: University Level and Reputation
学校层次与声誉.text: It is a 985 Project, 211 Project, and Double First-Class university. In the 2026 Soft Science Chinese University Ranking, it ranks 14th (Top 20), steadily rising from 19th in 2021, indicating continuously enhanced comprehensive strength and recognition. During the 2026 college entrance examination cycle, the reputation evaluation was slightly negative, involving 3 negative incidents related to teacher ethics and conduct, but the university handled them quickly and responded promptly, with limited impact on public opinion. Douyin popularity ranking is 47th (relatively hot), with medium to high exposure on short video platforms, and moderate application attention. No obvious enrollment gaps were recorded. In the fifth round of discipline evaluation, there are 12 disciplines rated A- or above, including Information and Communication Engineering and Control Science and Engineering as A+, and Computer Science and Technology, Mechanical Engineering, Materials Science and Engineering, and Optical Engineering as A. These are highly matched with the university's enrollment majors such as Electronic Information Experimental Class, Information Science and Technology, and Intelligent Manufacturing, providing strong appeal for related major applications.

专业热度与地域.title: Major Popularity and Region
专业热度与地域.text: Majors such as Electronic Information/Communication, Computer Science, and Electrical Engineering and Automation are highly recommended/recommended in 2024–2026, consistent with BIT's advantageous disciplines. In 2026, the number of Physics Category candidates in Hunan Province is approximately 347,000, an increase of about 8.4% compared to 320,192 in 2025, intensifying competition. Beijing Institute of Technology is located in Beijing, a first-tier city university, which is highly attractive to cross-province candidates. The university's enrollment proportion in Hunan Province is relatively small (about 2.5% of total enrollment in recent years), belonging to out-of-province enrollment with no local preference.

预测结论.title: Prediction Conclusion
预测结论.text: Considering the continuous forward trend of historical rankings, the unchanged 2026 enrollment plan, the approximately 8.4% increase in Physics Category candidates in Hunan Province, and the continuously rising university level, based on the 2025 baseline of 1269, it is expected that competition will slightly intensify in 2026, with the ranking moving slightly backward. The predicted minimum ranking is approximately 1299, with a range of 1169–1429.

major_tier_recommendation_en.overall_advice: If the ranking is within the predicted range of 1169–1429, it is recommended to focus on Tier 2 majors as the main choice, aim for Tier 1 majors as a stretch, and use Tier 3 majors as a safety net. If the ranking is significantly better than the lower limit of the range (<1169), focus on impacting Tier 1 flagship majors such as Xu Teli Elite Class and Future Precision Engineering Technology. If the ranking is close to the upper limit of the range (>1429), mainly rely on Tier 3 majors as a safety net.

历年位次与招生计划.title: Historical Rankings and Enrollment Plans
历年位次与招生计划.text: From 2021 to 2025, the minimum ranking for History Category was approximately 943 → 856 → 805 → 911 → 824, fluctuating within the 800–950 range without a clear unilateral trend; the 2025 baseline is about 824. The enrollment plan for this university in Hunan Province for History Category was 12 students in 2021, 11 in 2022, 11 in 2023, 20 in 2024, 16 in 2025, and 18 in 2026, with an increase of 2 students (+12.5%) in 2026 compared to 2025, a limited expansion.

学校层次与声誉.title: University Level and Reputation
学校层次与声誉.text: A 985 Project, 211 Project, and Double First-Class university; ranked 14th in the 2026 Soft Science China University Rankings, belonging to the top national science and engineering universities, with strong brand appeal to liberal arts candidates; the reputation evaluation during the 2026 college entrance examination cycle is slightly negative, but mainly involves science and engineering-related events, with relatively limited impact on liberal arts admissions; Douyin popularity ranking is 47th (relatively hot), with moderate overall exposure; BIT has relatively few liberal arts-related disciplines, with the Social Science Experimental Class covering economics, law, social work, etc., among which Business Administration is rated A- and Management Science and Engineering is rated A, indicating moderate strength in liberal arts disciplines.

专业热度与地域.title: Major Popularity and Region
专业热度与地域.text: The Social Science Experimental Class (Humanities and Social Sciences Direction/Management and Economics Direction) has generally lukewarm social popularity; in 2026, the number of History Category candidates in Hunan Province is approximately 187,000, an increase of about 31.2% from 142,547 in 2025, significantly intensifying competition; Beijing, as a first-tier city, has strong appeal to liberal arts candidates, but BIT offers limited liberal arts majors (only the Social Science Experimental Class), restricting some candidates' willingness to apply.

预测结论.title: Prediction Conclusion
预测结论.text: Considering factors such as a 12.5% enrollment expansion in 2026, a 31.2% increase in History Category candidates, and limited liberal arts major options, the ranking-back effect from enrollment expansion is insufficient to offset the intensified competition from the increase in candidates; based on the 2025 baseline of 824, the ranking is expected to shift slightly backward; the predicted minimum ranking is approximately 906, with a range of 815–997.
